status_notes: Initial research from Azure Policy documentation, 2026-04-04. Pass 1 (service docs). Needs Pass 2 (API), Pass 3 (cross-reference), and Pass 5 (human review with live Azure environment).
notes: Policy-as-code for Azure resource governance. Enforce standards, assess compliance, remediate at scale.

data.description: Individual policy rules that define conditions and effects for Azure resources. Built-in definitions (900+ maintained by Microsoft) cover common compliance scenarios. Custom definitions can be authored in JSON. Each policy has an effect that determines what happens when conditions are met.

options.description: Generate a compliance event but don't block the operation. Resource is flagged as non-compliant.
Block the resource operation. Prevents creation or modification that violates the policy.
Add additional fields to the resource during creation or update (e.g., add tags, require HTTPS).
Add, update, or remove properties on a resource (e.g., add tags, enforce settings).
Deploy a related resource if it doesn't exist (e.g., deploy diagnostic settings, deploy monitoring agents).
Audit if a related resource doesn't exist (e.g., check if diagnostic settings are configured).
Policy is not evaluated. Used to temporarily disable without removing assignment.

data.vendor_recommendation: Start with Audit effect to assess impact before enabling Deny. Use built-in definitions when possible. Create custom definitions for organization-specific requirements.

configure.cli: az policy definition create --name {name} --display-name '{display}' --rules file://rules.json --params file://params.json

match_nodes.description: Apply policies to scopes (management group, subscription, resource group)

data.description: Bind policy definitions or initiatives to a scope (management group, subscription, or resource group). Assignments determine which resources are evaluated. Supports exclusions for specific child scopes. Parameters can be customized per assignment. Enforcement mode controls whether the policy actively enforces or only audits.

options.description: Applies to all subscriptions and resource groups within the management group
Applies to all resource groups within the subscription
Applies to all resources within the resource group
DoNotEnforce allows compliance assessment without blocking operations

data.vendor_recommendation: Assign policies at the highest applicable scope (management group for organization-wide policies). Use DoNotEnforce mode to test impact before enabling enforcement. Use exclusions sparingly.

match_nodes.description: Groups of policy definitions for compliance frameworks

data.description: Collections of related policy definitions grouped together for a specific compliance goal. Also called policy set definitions. Built-in initiatives include major compliance frameworks. Custom initiatives can be created to group organization-specific policies. Assigned as a single unit to simplify management.

options.description: Microsoft's security best practices aligned with common compliance frameworks
Center for Internet Security benchmark for Azure
US government security and privacy controls
International information security management standard
Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard
Group your own policy definitions into a custom initiative

data.vendor_recommendation: Use built-in initiatives for standard compliance frameworks. Create custom initiatives for organization-specific requirements. Assign initiatives rather than individual policies for manageability.

audit.console: Azure Portal → Policy → Definitions → Filter by Type: Initiative

data.description: Continuous evaluation of resources against assigned policies. Resources are evaluated on creation, update, policy assignment changes, and periodic re-evaluation (default every 24 hours). Compliance states: Compliant, Non-compliant, Exempt, Conflicting, Not started. Compliance data is available at resource, policy, subscription, and management group levels.

options.description: Resource meets all applicable policy conditions
Resource violates one or more policy conditions
Resource has an active exemption from the policy
Two or more policies with conflicting rules apply to the resource
Evaluation has not yet occurred for this resource

data.vendor_recommendation: Monitor compliance state regularly. Set up alerts for non-compliance. Use compliance data to prioritize remediation. Export compliance data for audit reporting.

configure.note: Compliance evaluation is automatic. Manual trigger-scan forces immediate re-evaluation.

data.description: Remediation tasks bring non-compliant resources into compliance by executing the policy's DeployIfNotExists or Modify effect. Remediation uses a managed identity to make changes to resources. Tasks can target existing non-compliant resources (remediation) or be triggered automatically for new non-compliant resources.

options.description: One-time task to remediate existing non-compliant resources. Manually triggered.
Automatically create remediation tasks when non-compliance is detected. Configured in policy assignment.

default.note: Remediation tasks must be explicitly created. Auto-remediation must be enabled per policy assignment.

data.vendor_recommendation: Use remediation tasks for DeployIfNotExists policies (e.g., deploy diagnostic settings). Configure auto-remediation for well-tested policies. Ensure the managed identity has sufficient permissions.

match_nodes.description: Temporarily or permanently exempt resources from policy evaluation

data.description: Exempt specific resources or scopes from policy evaluation. Two categories: Waiver (non-compliant but approved exception — resource is still tracked as non-compliant for reporting) and Mitigated (compliance achieved through alternative means — resource is counted as compliant). Exemptions can be time-limited with expiration dates.

options.description: Resource is non-compliant but exception is approved. Still reported as non-compliant in compliance data.
Compliance is achieved through alternative means. Counted as compliant in compliance data.

data.vendor_recommendation: Use exemptions sparingly and with documented justification. Set expiration dates for temporary exemptions. Review active exemptions regularly. Use Mitigated only when alternative controls are genuinely in place.
